reason: The text describes a conflict between the general population (עַם - people) and the leadership elite consisting of Aaron (the high priest) and Moses (the political/spiritual leader). The people collectively gather and make demands, creating the golden calf and establishing their own religious practice independent of the leadership. This represents tension between the populace and the ruling figures. While the text doesn't explicitly use the term 'workers,' the general population would have consisted largely of laborers in the ancient context, making the characterization reasonable.
